rows.notes: Top title line in Latin. Handwriting of the surname is unclear (looks like 'Roguembe' or similar). No monetary amounts on this line; label at page head. Column alignment not needed for header.
Clear date line 'a Secundo die mensis Novembris 1743.' Legible Latin; no amounts present.
Clear Latin line 'ad eundem Diem Revolution 1744.' No monetary amounts.
Section heading 'Arrearagia Recepta' (receipts of arrears). Underlined; no monetary amounts. Handwriting clear.
Column heading for currency at top-right ('L S D'). No amounts. Alignment of currency columns established from nearby totals.
Clear entry 'Gwinear'. Numeric columns line up with vertical rules; amount reads 100 pounds, 00 shillings, 00 pence exactly.

Description slightly uncertain (handwritten place-name spelling). Amount columns clear: '22' in pounds column, '07' shillings, '04' pence with a trailing 'ob' / loop interpreted as half-penny (0.5). Column alignment unambiguous.
Place name and 'Rect' (rectory) clearly legible. Amount '30 00 00' is unambiguous and validates (shillings 0, pence 0).

Description 'Kidlington Rect' clear. Pounds column reads '60' (the tens digit is distinct from the 50/40 style above). Alignment with neighboring rows consistent. I read 60 pounds rather than 50; shape of the '6' is clear.
Entry reads 'Yarnton Rect. pro 4 Ann.' Amount '40 00 00' is clearly aligned and legible.
Text reads 'Yarnton Tenem' or 'Tenent' pro 4 An. Amount '34 17 10' is well aligned; shillings 17 and pence 10 are within valid ranges.
Clear 'Merton Rect'. Amount '50 00 00' is unambiguous.
Name reading '(al. Bigg)' somewhat uncertain but legible enough; amount '01 13 06' reads clearly in the columns. Pence shows as '06' without fraction.

Surname uncertain (looks like 'Barrett' but faint). Amount shows '00' pounds, '03' shillings, '04' pence with a small looping mark interpreted as half-penny (0.5). The fractional symbol is faint; interpretation as 1/2d is plausible.

Entry 'Kidlington Mills pro Anno' clear. Amount reads '07 18 10' with a small superscript '3/4' mark after the pence — interpreted as three farthings (0.75). Columns align well and values validate (shillings 18 <20).
Entry 'Minn pro Anno' (name slightly abbreviated on page). Amount '01 03 04' is clear and well-aligned.
Reads 'Ledenhall (now Jesus Coll)'. Amount '00 10 00' is clear (10 shillings).
Entry 'Franklin pro Anno' legible; amount '01 00 00' clearly aligned.
Reads 'Bayister (now Franklin)'. Amount '00 15 00' (15 shillings) clear.
Single-word entry 'Sadler' clear. Amount reads '04 01 03' but the ink is slightly lighter than earlier lines; chosen digits align with the pounds/shillings/pence columns.

Description 'Clifton Ferry' legible. Pounds clearly '04'. The shillings digits are ambiguous on the scan (looks like '64' but 64 is invalid for shillings); interpreted as '06' shillings based on likely handwriting of '6' and validation (6 < 20). Pence reads '00'. This is a moderate-confidence decision due to ambiguity in the middle digit.
Reads 'South Newinton Tenent' (tenement). Amount '02 14 05' aligns well with the column rules and is legible.
Place-name spelling uncertain on the manuscript (read as 'Ladlane' or similar). Amount '17 13 04' is fairly clear in the columns, though the description requires interpretation.
Entry reads like 'Midenham' (spelling uncertain). Amount '06 13 04' appears in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; digits are slightly faded but readable and valid (shillings 13).
Reads 'Wells pro 4 parte An.' Amount '01 00 00' is clear and aligns with the vertical rules.
Entry 'Kidlington Capons & Brown' legible. Amount '02 15 00' is clearly written and validates.
Last ledger entry reads like 'Minkennitt pro Anno' (spelling variant). Amount '20 00 00' is clear in pounds column.

Final total on the page shown with pound sign '£' and pence fraction '3/4' clearly visible. Vertical alignment of this total was used to set the pounds/shillings/pence column boundaries. Total legible though a few description spellings above were uncertain.
